1. SCOPE 
This document covers the stress analysis of the pipes of the TES heat exchangers of the 
SOLANA GENERATION STATION. 
It also includes the nozzle load verification of the heat exchanger nozzles. 
 
This calculation shows the calculation for one train but it is the same for the rest of trains. 
2. CONCLUSIONS 
The piping system complies with the requirements of the B31.3 piping code ed. 2014. 
 
All equipment nozzles stresses have been verified according Sect. VIII div 2 rules, using Finite 
Element Analysis. 
3. STANDARDS AND TECHNICAL REFERENCE DOCUMENTATION 
3.1. PROJECT DOCUMENTATION 
4521-PLN-AEE-68-71-P278-GAR Rev.00 TES HEX Train P&ID (20/10/2016) 
4521-CAL-LOI-039-062-0002-GAR Rev00 Process Flow Conditions Heat & Mass Balances 
4521-ESP-ANC-00-62-0001 Project Design Criteria 
4521-ESP-ANC-68-61-CS30 CS30 Piping Specification 
4521-ESP-ANC-68-61-CS31 CS31 Piping Specification 
4521-PLN-LOI-039-062-0004-GAR Rev00 Layout 
4521-PLN-LOI-039-062-0001-GAR General arrangement and loads 
3.2. INTERNATIONAL STANDARDS 
ASME B31 Piping code section 3 Process Piping 2014 edition 
ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code Section VIII Division 2 
 
3.3. OTHER DOCUMENTS 
AutoPIPE CONNECT ver. 10.01 Reference manual 
AutoPIPE CONNECT ver.10.01 Modelling approach manual

4. DESIGN CONDITIONS 
4.1. SITE DATA 
The plant site is located in Maricopa County, Arizona, approximately 80 miles southwest of 
Phoenix, and 20 miles west of the town on Gila Bend near Interstate 8 and Painted Rock Dam 
Road. 
City Gila Bend, Arizona 
Elevation (feet above sea level) 735 ft 
Dry Bulb Temperature (design °F) 122°F 
Dry Bulb Temperature (design min, °F) 25°F 
Wet Bulb Temperature (design max °F) 72°F 
Barometric Pressure (psia) 14.30 
Building Code IBC-2006 
All Other Building & Structure Classification (u.n.o.) TBD 
Location Lat. 32.92 ° 
 Long. -112.98 ° 
Basic Wind Speed 90 MPH 
4.2. CALCULATION PRESSURE AND TEMPERATURE 
The pressure and temperature for pipe stress calculation will be the design conditions. 
Loads on nozzles will be calculated using the operating conditions. 
4.3. LOADS CONSIDERED IN THIS ANALYSIS 
4.3.1. PRIMARY LOADS 
The self weight of pipe, insulation and contents will be included in the analysis, also the 
internal pressure. 
4.3.2. THERMAL LOADS 
The thermal loads are caused by: 
 Thermal dilatation of the metal of the pipe due to the inner fluid temperature or ambient 
temperature. 
 Imposed displacements at the pipe connection to equipment or other pipes. 
 Friction forces in the pipe su pports, produced by the movement of the support due to pipe 
thermal displacements. 
4.3.3. OCCASIONAL LOADS 
The occasional loads are produced by eventual effects such as wind, seismic movements, 
valve sudden opening and/or closing, hydrostatic test, etc. 
 
Wind loads are calculated per ASCE 7-05. 
Basic wind speed is 90 mph. 

[page 7]
ABENGOA 
Document: 4521-CAL-LOI-039-062-0003-GAR 
 
Revision: 00 Date: 07/12/2016 
Page: 7 of 59 
 
Wind exposure category: C 
Wind Importace Factor: 1.15 
Gust Effect Factor: G = 0.85 
Base of equipment is assumed to be at 10 ft above grade level. 
 
Hydrostatic test pressure will be according to article 345.4.2 of B31.3. 
 
Earthquake loads are calculated per ASCE 7-05. 
 
 
Seismic response spectrum will be included in both horizontal orthogonal directions, and in 
vertical direction with a factor of 0.7. 
 
4.3.4. IMPOSED DISPLACEMENTS 
No imposed displacements are included in this analysis. It is assumed that the equipment 
foundations will be common or the settlement (if any) will be the same for the three groups 
of heat exchangers. 
5. B31.3 CODE SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS 
The stresses in piping systems under the scope of the B31.3 piping code shall comply the 
following requirements: 
 
5.1. PRIMARY STRESSES 
The primary stresses SL shall be less than the basic allowable stress of the material SH, being:

Fa is the longitudinal force caused by primary loads. 
Ap is the cross sectional area of metal 
Mi and Mo are the in-plane and out-of-plane moments caused by primary loads. 
Z is the section modulus. 
Mt is the torsional moment caused by primary loads. 
I is the stress index. I ≥ 1. 
 
5.2. OCCASIONAL STRESSES 
The occasional stresses SLO shall meet: 
 
 
 
The terms are the same as for the sustained stress equations, calculated with the appropriate 
loads (primary and occasional). 
5.3. DISPLACEMENT STRESSES 
The effect of thermal expansion and other cyclic loads shall meet:

6. COMPUTER CALCULATION 
The computer calculation is done using AutoPIPE CONNECT ver. 10.01, developed and 
maintained by Bentley Systems Inc. 
The orientation of the calculation model is North = -X. The vertical up axe is +Y. 
 
6.1. LOAD SEQUENCE 
AutoPIPE works by load sequence, which means that the effects of the different acting loads 
over the system are evaluated in the order in which they are applied. In this way, the first 
calculation is made for the gravity loads, and over the deformed system, the pressure loads 
are applied, then the temperature actions over the pressure state. These final forces, 
moments and displacements represent the system state under operation or design, according 
to the applied loads. 
 
The occasional loads are applied over the nominal discharge state. 
 
The loads are: 
 
 GR – gravity loads, includes self weight, content weight, insulation weight and other additional 
weights. 
 P1, T1 to P4, T4 – pressure and temperature for operating condition 1 to 4. 
 P5, T5 – pressure and temperature for minimum ambient temperature. 
 P6, T6 – pressure and temperature for design condition 
 W1 – wind pressure in +X direction (North) 
 W2 – wind pressure in –X direction (South) 
 W3 – wind pressure in +Z direction (East) 
 W4 – wind pressure in –Z direction (West) 
 R1 – Seismic response spectrum analysis. 
To guarantee the code compliance calculations, there will be separated analysis sets, that are 
independent calculations, using cold modulus of elasticity for stress calculation, and hot 
modulus of elasticity for loads on nozzles. 
 
6.2. STRESS COMBINATIONS 
The combinations for stress evaluation are calculated using the cold modulus of elasticity and 
pressure stiffness. The pressure elongation effects are not included. 
 
Some combinations are predefined and are used in other combinations: 

[page 10]
ABENGOA 
Document: 4521-CAL-LOI-039-062-0003-GAR 
 
Revision: 00 Date: 07/12/2016 
Page: 10 of 59 
 
 
 (SUS) is the maximum sustained stress. 
 (LONG) is the pressure longitudinal stress. 
 (EXP) is the maximum expansion stress. 
 (HOOP) is the hoop stress. 
 (OCC) is the maximum occasional stress. 
 
The code compliance combinations are 
 
 Sustained stress: GR + (LONG) 
 Expansion stress: Amb to Tx, where x means the operating case, 1 for design, from 2 to 13 for 
operating cases. 
 Maximum range: is the maximum expansion stress between the different operating cases, that 
is from T2 to T3, T2 to T4, etc.. 
 Occasional seismic stress: (SUS) + E1 … (SUS) + E8 
 Occasional wind stress: (SUS) + W1 … (SUS) + W4 
 Hydrotest stress: GR + P1 + (LONG), using 1.5 times P1. 
 
6.3. FORCES, MOMENTS AND DISPLACEMENTS 
 
The combinations for forces, moments and displacements are calculated using the hot 
modulus of elasticity and including the pressure elongation effects. 
 
The combinations are: 
 
 Weight: GR 
 Operation: GRP1T1 to GRP4T4 
 Minimum temperature: GRP5T5 
 Design: GRP6T6 
 Operation + wind: GRP1T1W1 to GRP1T1W4 
 Operation + seismic: GRP1T1 ± R1

ABENGOA

13. NOZZLE LOADS VERIFICATION 
The nozzle load verification is performed using the corroded thickness for both shell and 
nozzle. 
This chapter contains the maximum forces and moment applied to the nozzles and the stress 
summary in tabulated form for each nozzle. 
The complete NozzlePRO output reports are in Annex G.

 differences in temperature and/or thermal expansion coefficients.

 cases. The design pressure is used for primary cases. The ratio 
 of the operating/design pressure = 0.741 

[page 163]
 No external forces or bending moments were included in this analysis.

 The nozzle offset from the head centerline has been adjusted to 
 a value of 30.880 in. so that it will better fit on 
 the head. Please check the input carefully, and adjust the 
 design accordingly for this assumption.

 Nozzle Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Zero Degree Orientation Vector: 0.000 1.000 0.000 
 The location of the nozzle/plate on the head is being moved 
 slightly toward the middle of the head to allow for better 
 meshing near the outside radius of the head. The original 
 centerline location was 30.880 and the new 
 location is 29.643.

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 3:27pm DEC 2,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.7406250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 Stress Intensification Factors 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 3:27pm DEC 2,2016 
 Stress Intensification Factors $X 
 Branch/Nozzle Sif Summary 
 Peak Primary Secondary SSI 
 Axial : 8.421 4.242 12.475 2.008 
 Inplane : 7.662 3.127 11.351 2.423 
 Outplane: 7.342 3.098 10.877 1.946 
 Torsion : 0.717 1.046 1.062 1.041 
 Pressure: 1.027 1.043 1.521 1.237 
 The above stress intensification factors are to be used 
 in a beam-type analysis of the piping system. Inplane, 
 Outplane and Torsional sif's should be used with the 
 matching branch pipe whose diameter and thickness is given 
 below. The axial sif should be used to intensify the 
 axial stress in the branch pipe calculated by F/A. The 
 pressure sif should be used to intensify the nominal 
 pressure stress in the PARENT or HEADER, calculated 
 from PDo/2T. B31 calculations use mean diameters and 
 Section VIII calculations use outside diameters. SSIs are 
 based on peak stress factors and correlated test results. 
 Pipe OD : 24.000 in. 
 Pipe Thk: 0.961 in. 
 Z approx: 400.627 cu.in. 
 Z exact : 385.254 cu.in. 
 (SSI = SIF^x) Axial Inpl Outpl Tors Pres 
 SIF/SSI Exponents: 0.678 0.560 0.567 -0.135 1.624 
 SIF/SSI exponent based on relationship between primary and 
 peak stress factors from the finite element analysis. 
 B31.3 Branch Pressure i-factor = 9.584 
 Header Pressure i-factor = 2.072 
 The B31.3 pressure i-factors should be used with with 
 F/A, where F is the axial force due to pressure, and 
 A is the area of the pipe wall. This is equivalent to 
 finding the pressure stress from (ip)(PD/4T).

 Allowable Loads 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 3:27pm DEC 2,2016 
 Allowable Loads $X 
 SECONDARY Maximum Conservative Realistic 
 Load Type (Range): Individual Simultaneous Simultaneous 
 Occuring Occuring Occuring 
 Axial Force (lb. ) 284914. 46542. 69813. 
 Inplane Moment (in. lb.) 1734309. 200329. 424962. 
 Outplane Moment (in. lb.) 1809977. 209069. 443503. 
 Torsional Moment (in. lb.) 18535444. 2928913. 4393370. 
 Pressure (psi ) 608.42 320.00 320.00 
 PRIMARY Maximum Conservative Realistic 
 Load Type: Individual Simultaneous Simultaneous 
 Occuring Occuring Occuring 
 Axial Force (lb. ) 458701. 61823. 92734. 
 Inplane Moment (in. lb.) 3412695. 325239. 689935. 
 Outplane Moment (in. lb.) 3445134. 328330. 696493. 
 Torsional Moment (in. lb.) 11936366. 1608762. 2413143. 
 Pressure (psi ) 537.21 320.00 320.00 
 NOTES: 
 1) Maximum Individual Occuring Loads are the maximum 
 allowed values of the respective loads if all other 
 load components are zero, i.e. the listed axial force 
 may be applied if the inplane, outplane and torsional 
 moments, and the pressure are zero. 
 2) The Conservative Allowable Simultaneous loads are 
 the maximum loads that can be applied simultaneously. 
 A conservative stress combination equation is used 
 that typically produces stresses within 50-70% of the 
 allowable stress. 
 3) The Realistic Allowable Simultaneous loads are the 
 maximum loads that can be applied simultaneously. A 
 more realistic stress combination equation is used 
 based on experience at Paulin Research. Stresses are 
 typically produced within 80-105% of the allowable. 
 4) Secondary allowable loads are limits for expansion 
 and operating piping loads. 
 5) Primary allowable loads are limits for weight, 
 primary and sustained type piping loads. 
Table of Contents
 Flexibilities 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 3:27pm DEC 2,2016 
 Flexibilities $X 
 The following stiffnesses should be used in a piping, 
 "beam-type" analysis of the intersection. The stiff- 
 nesses should be inserted at the surface of the 
 branch/header or nozzle/vessel junction. The general 
 characteristics used for the branch pipe should be: 
 Outside Diameter = 24.000 in. 
 Wall Thickness = 0.961 in. 
 Axial Translational Stiffness = 7538372. lb./in. 
 Inplane Rotational Stiffness = 12516742. in.lb./deg 
 Outplane Rotational Stiffness = 12411282. in.lb./deg 
 Torsional Rotational Stiffness = 204487632. in.lb./deg 
 Intersection Flexibility Factors for Branch/Nozzle : 
 Find axial stiffness: K = 3EI/(kd)^3 lb./in. 
 Find bending and torsional stiffnesses: K = EI/(kd) in.lb.per radian. 
 The EI product is 0.13407E+12 lb.in.^2 
 The value of (d) to use is: 23.039 in.. 
 The resulting bending stiffness is in units of force x length per radian. 
 Axial Flexibility Factor (k) = 1.634 
 Inplane Flexibility Factor (k) = 8.114 

[page 173]
 Outplane Flexibility Factor (k) = 8.183 
 Torsional Flexibility Factor (k) = 0.497

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 3:40pm DEC 2,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 

[page 204]
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.4308943 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 The above stress intensification factors are to be used 
 in a beam-type analysis of the piping system. Inplane, 
 Outplane and Torsional sif's should be used with the 
 matching branch pipe whose diameter and thickness is given 
 below. The axial sif should be used to intensify the 
 axial stress in the branch pipe calculated by F/A. The 
 pressure sif should be used to intensify the nominal 
 pressure stress in the PARENT or HEADER, calculated 
 from PDo/2T. B31 calculations use mean diameters and 
 Section VIII calculations use outside diameters. SSIs are 
 based on peak stress factors and correlated test results. 
 Pipe OD : 24.000 in. 
 Pipe Thk: 1.811 in. 
 Z approx: 700.299 cu.in. 
 Z exact : 651.768 cu.in. 
 (SSI = SIF^x) Axial Inpl Outpl Tors Pres 
 SIF/SSI Exponents: 0.753 0.517 0.793 2.485 0.625 
 SIF/SSI exponent based on relationship between primary and 
 peak stress factors from the finite element analysis. 
 B31.3 Branch Pressure i-factor = 25.163 
 Header Pressure i-factor = 3.179 
 The B31.3 pressure i-factors should be used with with 
 F/A, where F is the axial force due to pressure, and 
 A is the area of the pipe wall. This is equivalent to 
 finding the pressure stress from (ip)(PD/4T).

 Allowable Loads 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 3:40pm DEC 2,2016 
 Allowable Loads $X 
 SECONDARY Maximum Conservative Realistic 
 Load Type (Range): Individual Simultaneous Simultaneous 
 Occuring Occuring Occuring 
 Axial Force (lb. ) 278168. 38862. 58293. 
 Inplane Moment (in. lb.) 1916757. 189351. 401675. 
 Outplane Moment (in. lb.) 1519949. 150152. 318520. 
 Torsional Moment (in. lb.) 21446568. 3070753. 4606130. 
 Pressure (psi ) 550.54 319.80 319.80 
 PRIMARY Maximum Conservative Realistic 
 Load Type: Individual Simultaneous Simultaneous 
 Occuring Occuring Occuring 
 Axial Force (lb. ) 759658. 169034. 253551. 
 Inplane Moment (in. lb.) 9144735. 1352012. 2868050. 
 Outplane Moment (in. lb.) 3645054. 538906. 1143193. 
 Torsional Moment (in. lb.) 19331258. 4041889. 6062834. 
 Pressure (psi ) 756.92 319.80 319.80 
 NOTES: 
 1) Maximum Individual Occuring Loads are the maximum 
 allowed values of the respective loads if all other 
 load components are zero, i.e. the listed axial force 
 may be applied if the inplane, outplane and torsional 
 moments, and the pressure are zero. 
 2) The Conservative Allowable Simultaneous loads are 
 the maximum loads that can be applied simultaneously. 
 A conservative stress combination equation is used 
 that typically produces stresses within 50-70% of the 
 allowable stress. 
 3) The Realistic Allowable Simultaneous loads are the 
 maximum loads that can be applied simultaneously. A 
 more realistic stress combination equation is used 
 based on experience at Paulin Research. Stresses are 
 typically produced within 80-105% of the allowable. 
 4) Secondary allowable loads are limits for expansion 
 and operating piping loads. 
 5) Primary allowable loads are limits for weight, 
 primary and sustained type piping loads. 
Table of Contents
 Flexibilities 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 3:40pm DEC 2,2016 
 Flexibilities $X 
 The following stiffnesses should be used in a piping, 
 "beam-type" analysis of the intersection. The stiff- 
 nesses should be inserted at the surface of the 
 branch/header or nozzle/vessel junction. The general 
 characteristics used for the branch pipe should be: 
 Outside Diameter = 24.000 in. 
 Wall Thickness = 1.811 in. 
 Axial Translational Stiffness = 5707137. lb./in. 
 Inplane Rotational Stiffness = 13332864. in.lb./deg 
 Outplane Rotational Stiffness = 5345162. in.lb./deg 
 Torsional Rotational Stiffness = 236040256. in.lb./deg 
 Intersection Flexibility Factors for Branch/Nozzle : 
 Find axial stiffness: K = 3EI/(kd)^3 lb./in. 
 Find bending and torsional stiffnesses: K = EI/(kd) in.lb.per radian. 
 The EI product is 0.21743E+12 lb.in.^2 
 The value of (d) to use is: 22.189 in.. 

[page 208]
 The resulting bending stiffness is in units of force x length per radian. 
 Axial Flexibility Factor (k) = 2.187 
 Inplane Flexibility Factor (k) = 12.827 
 Outplane Flexibility Factor (k) = 31.996 
 Torsional Flexibility Factor (k) = 0.725

[page 397]
 Thermal Stresses will be introduced into this analysis due to the 
 differences in temperature and/or thermal expansion coefficients. 
 The following temperatures have been specified for the analysis: 
 Nozzle Inside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Nozzle Outside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Vessel Inside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Vessel Outside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Nozzle Pressure : 320.0 psi 
 Vessel Pressure : 320.0 psi 
 Operating Pressure : 237.0 psi 
 The operating pressure is used for secondary and peak stress 
 cases. The design pressure is used for primary cases. The ratio 
 of the operating/design pressure = 0.741 
 No external forces or bending moments were included in this analysis. 
 Stresses ARE nodally AVERAGED. 
 The nozzle offset from the head centerline has been adjusted to 
 a value of 30.942 in. so that it will better fit on 
 the head. Please check the input carefully, and adjust the 
 design accordingly for this assumption. 
 Vessel Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Nozzle Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Zero Degree Orientation Vector: 0.000 1.000 0.000 
 The location of the nozzle/plate on the head is being moved 
 slightly toward the middle of the head to allow for better 
 meshing near the outside radius of the head. The original 
 centerline location was 30.942 and the new 
 location is 29.705.

 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 1:15pm DEC 5,2016 
 Load Case Report $X 
 Inner and outer element temperatures are the same 
 throughout the model. No thermal ratcheting 
 calculations will be performed. 
 THE 6 LOAD CASES ANALYZED ARE: 
 1 WEIGHT ONLY (Wgt Only) 
 Weight ONLY case run to get the stress range 
 between the installed and the operating states. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 1 
 Loads due to Weight 
 2 SUSTAINED (Wgt+Pr) 
 Sustained case run to satisfy local primary 
 membrane and bending stress limits. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 2 
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 3 Thermal ONLY 
 Thermal ONLY case run in the event expansion 

[page 398]
 stresses exceed the secondary stress allowable. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 3 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Thermal Only) 
 4 OPERATING 
 Case run to compute the operating stresses used in 
 secondary, peak and range calculations as needed. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 4 
 Pressure Case 1 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Operating) 
 5 RANGE (Fatigue Calc Performed) 
 Case run to get the RANGE of stresses.

 /-------- Combinations in Range Case 5

 Minus Stress Results from CASE 1

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 1:16pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.7406250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 Model Type : Hemispherical Head

 to the head centerline : 37.863 in.

 Cold Allowable : 26700.0 psi 
 Hot Allowable : 14800.0 psi 
 Material DB # 7005318. 
 Ultimate Tensile (Amb) : 70000.0 psi 
 Yield Strength (Amb) : 40000.0 psi 
 Yield Strength (Hot) : 27700.0 psi 
 Elastic Modulus (Amb) : 29200000.0 psi 
 Poissons Ratio : 0.300 
 Expansion Coefficient : 0.7700E-05 in./in./deg. 
 Weight Density : 0.0000E+00 lb./cu.in. (NOT USED) 
 Design Operating Cycles : 16000. 
 Ambient Temperature (Deg.) : 70.00 
 Thermal Stresses will be introduced into this analysis due to the 
 differences in temperature and/or thermal expansion coefficients. 
 The following temperatures have been specified for the analysis: 
 Nozzle Inside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 

[page 433]
 Nozzle Outside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Vessel Inside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Vessel Outside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Nozzle Pressure : 320.0 psi 
 Vessel Pressure : 320.0 psi 
 Operating Pressure : 237.0 psi 
 The operating pressure is used for secondary and peak stress 
 cases. The design pressure is used for primary cases. The ratio 
 of the operating/design pressure = 0.741 
 No external forces or bending moments were included in this analysis. 
 Stresses ARE nodally AVERAGED. 
 The nozzle offset from the head centerline has been adjusted to 
 a value of 30.942 in. so that it will better fit on 
 the head. Please check the input carefully, and adjust the 
 design accordingly for this assumption. 
 Vessel Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Nozzle Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Zero Degree Orientation Vector: 0.000 1.000 0.000 
 The location of the nozzle/plate on the head is being moved 
 slightly toward the middle of the head to allow for better 
 meshing near the outside radius of the head. The original 
 centerline location was 30.942 and the new 
 location is 29.705.

 Load Case Report $X 
 Inner and outer element temperatures are the same 
 throughout the model. No thermal ratcheting 
 calculations will be performed. 
 THE 6 LOAD CASES ANALYZED ARE: 
 1 WEIGHT ONLY (Wgt Only) 
 Weight ONLY case run to get the stress range 
 between the installed and the operating states. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 1 
 Loads due to Weight 
 2 SUSTAINED (Wgt+Pr) 
 Sustained case run to satisfy local primary 
 membrane and bending stress limits. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 2 
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 3 Thermal ONLY 
 Thermal ONLY case run in the event expansion 
 stresses exceed the secondary stress allowable. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 3 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Thermal Only) 
 4 OPERATING 
 Case run to compute the operating stresses used in 
 secondary, peak and range calculations as needed. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 4 

[page 434]
 Pressure Case 1 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Operating) 
 5 RANGE (Fatigue Calc Performed) 
 Case run to get the RANGE of stresses. 
 as described in NB-3222.2, 5.5.3.2, 5.5.5.2 or 5.5.6.1. 
 /-------- Combinations in Range Case 5 
 Plus Stress Results from CASE 4 
 Minus Stress Results from CASE 1 
 6 OCCASIONAL

 requirements of the Code.

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 1:26pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.7406250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 LOCAL STRESSES CALCULATION 
 Model Type : Hemispherical Head 
 Parent Geometry

 Expansion Coefficient : 0.8000E-05 in./in./deg. 
 Weight Density : 0.0000E+00 lb./cu.in.(NOT USED)

 to the head centerline : 37.863 in. 
 Nozzle Tilt Angle : 60.000 deg. 
 Nozzle Properties 
 Cold Allowable : 26700.0 psi 
 Hot Allowable : 14800.0 psi 
 Material DB # 7005318. 
 Ultimate Tensile (Amb) : 70000.0 psi 
 Yield Strength (Amb) : 40000.0 psi 
 Yield Strength (Hot) : 27700.0 psi 
 Elastic Modulus (Amb) : 29200000.0 psi 
 Poissons Ratio : 0.300 
 Expansion Coefficient : 0.7700E-05 in./in./deg. 
 Weight Density : 0.0000E+00 lb./cu.in. (NOT USED) 
 Design Operating Cycles : 16000. 
 Ambient Temperature (Deg.) : 70.00 
 Thermal Stresses will be introduced into this analysis due to the 
 differences in temperature and/or thermal expansion coefficients. 
 The following temperatures have been specified for the analysis: 
 Nozzle Inside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 

[page 468]
 Nozzle Outside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Vessel Inside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Vessel Outside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Nozzle Pressure : 320.0 psi 
 Vessel Pressure : 320.0 psi 
 Operating Pressure : 237.0 psi 
 The operating pressure is used for secondary and peak stress 
 cases. The design pressure is used for primary cases. The ratio 
 of the operating/design pressure = 0.741 
 No external forces or bending moments were included in this analysis. 
 Stresses ARE nodally AVERAGED. 
 The nozzle offset from the head centerline has been adjusted to 
 a value of 30.942 in. so that it will better fit on 
 the head. Please check the input carefully, and adjust the 
 design accordingly for this assumption. 
 Vessel Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Nozzle Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Zero Degree Orientation Vector: 0.000 1.000 0.000 
 The location of the nozzle/plate on the head is being moved 
 slightly toward the middle of the head to allow for better 
 meshing near the outside radius of the head. The original 
 centerline location was 30.942 and the new 
 location is 29.705.

 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 1:38pm DEC 5,2016 
 Load Case Report $X 
 Inner and outer element temperatures are the same 
 throughout the model. No thermal ratcheting 
 calculations will be performed. 
 THE 6 LOAD CASES ANALYZED ARE: 
 1 WEIGHT ONLY (Wgt Only) 
 Weight ONLY case run to get the stress range 
 between the installed and the operating states. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 1 
 Loads due to Weight 
 2 SUSTAINED (Wgt+Pr) 
 Sustained case run to satisfy local primary 
 membrane and bending stress limits. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 2 
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 3 Thermal ONLY 
 Thermal ONLY case run in the event expansion 
 stresses exceed the secondary stress allowable. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 3 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Thermal Only) 
 4 OPERATING 
 Case run to compute the operating stresses used in 
 secondary, peak and range calculations as needed. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 4 

[page 469]
 Pressure Case 1 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Operating) 
 5 RANGE (Fatigue Calc Performed) 
 Case run to get the RANGE of stresses. 
 as described in NB-3222.2, 5.5.3.2, 5.5.5.2 or 5.5.6.1. 
 /-------- Combinations in Range Case 5 
 Plus Stress Results from CASE 4 
 Minus Stress Results from CASE 1 
 6 OCCASIONAL 
 Occasional load case established per the 
 requirements of the Code. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 6 
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 Loads from (Occasional)

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 1:39pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.7406250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 to the head centerline : 37.863 in.

 Nozzle Properties 
 Cold Allowable : 26700.0 psi 
 Hot Allowable : 14800.0 psi 
 Material DB # 7005318. 
 Ultimate Tensile (Amb) : 70000.0 psi 
 Yield Strength (Amb) : 40000.0 psi 
 Yield Strength (Hot) : 27700.0 psi 
 Elastic Modulus (Amb) : 29200000.0 psi 
 Poissons Ratio : 0.300 
 Expansion Coefficient : 0.7700E-05 in./in./deg. 
 Weight Density : 0.0000E+00 lb./cu.in. (NOT USED) 
 Design Operating Cycles : 16000. 
 Ambient Temperature (Deg.) : 70.00 
 Thermal Stresses will be introduced into this analysis due to the 
 differences in temperature and/or thermal expansion coefficients. 
 The following temperatures have been specified for the analysis: 
 Nozzle Inside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 

[page 503]
 Nozzle Outside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Vessel Inside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Vessel Outside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Nozzle Pressure : 320.0 psi 
 Vessel Pressure : 320.0 psi 
 Operating Pressure : 237.0 psi 
 The operating pressure is used for secondary and peak stress 
 cases. The design pressure is used for primary cases. The ratio 
 of the operating/design pressure = 0.741 
 No external forces or bending moments were included in this analysis. 
 Stresses ARE nodally AVERAGED. 
 The nozzle offset from the head centerline has been adjusted to 
 a value of 30.942 in. so that it will better fit on 
 the head. Please check the input carefully, and adjust the 
 design accordingly for this assumption. 
 Vessel Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Nozzle Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Zero Degree Orientation Vector: 0.000 1.000 0.000 
 The location of the nozzle/plate on the head is being moved 
 slightly toward the middle of the head to allow for better 
 meshing near the outside radius of the head. The original 
 centerline location was 30.942 and the new 
 location is 29.705.

 Load Case Report 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 1:47pm DEC 5,2016 
 Load Case Report $X 
 Inner and outer element temperatures are the same 
 throughout the model. No thermal ratcheting 
 calculations will be performed. 
 THE 6 LOAD CASES ANALYZED ARE: 
 1 WEIGHT ONLY (Wgt Only) 
 Weight ONLY case run to get the stress range 
 between the installed and the operating states. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 1 
 Loads due to Weight 
 2 SUSTAINED (Wgt+Pr) 
 Sustained case run to satisfy local primary 
 membrane and bending stress limits. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 2 
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 3 Thermal ONLY 
 Thermal ONLY case run in the event expansion 
 stresses exceed the secondary stress allowable. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 3 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Thermal Only) 
 4 OPERATING 
 Case run to compute the operating stresses used in 
 secondary, peak and range calculations as needed. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 4 

[page 504]
 Pressure Case 1 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Operating) 
 5 RANGE (Fatigue Calc Performed) 
 Case run to get the RANGE of stresses. 
 as described in NB-3222.2, 5.5.3.2, 5.5.5.2 or 5.5.6.1. 
 /-------- Combinations in Range Case 5 
 Plus Stress Results from CASE 4 
 Minus Stress Results from CASE 1 
 6 OCCASIONAL 
 Occasional load case established per the 
 requirements of the Code. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 6 
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 Loads from (Occasional)

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 1:48pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.7406250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 Material DB # 7005318. 
 Ultimate Tensile (Amb) : 70000.0 psi 
 Yield Strength (Amb) : 40000.0 psi 
 Yield Strength (Hot) : 27700.0 psi 
 Elastic Modulus (Amb) : 29200000.0 psi 
 Poissons Ratio : 0.300 
 Expansion Coefficient : 0.7700E-05 in./in./deg. 
 Weight Density : 0.0000E+00 lb./cu.in. (NOT USED) 
 Design Operating Cycles : 16000. 
 Ambient Temperature (Deg.) : 70.00 
 Thermal Stresses will be introduced into this analysis due to the 
 differences in temperature and/or thermal expansion coefficients. 
 The following temperatures have been specified for the analysis: 
 Nozzle Inside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 

[page 538]
 Nozzle Outside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Vessel Inside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Vessel Outside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Nozzle Pressure : 320.0 psi 
 Vessel Pressure : 320.0 psi 
 Operating Pressure : 237.0 psi 
 The operating pressure is used for secondary and peak stress 
 cases. The design pressure is used for primary cases. The ratio 
 of the operating/design pressure = 0.741 
 No external forces or bending moments were included in this analysis. 
 Stresses ARE nodally AVERAGED. 
 The nozzle offset from the head centerline has been adjusted to 
 a value of 30.942 in. so that it will better fit on 
 the head. Please check the input carefully, and adjust the 
 design accordingly for this assumption. 
 Vessel Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Nozzle Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Zero Degree Orientation Vector: 0.000 1.000 0.000 
 The location of the nozzle/plate on the head is being moved 
 slightly toward the middle of the head to allow for better 
 meshing near the outside radius of the head. The original 
 centerline location was 30.942 and the new 
 location is 29.705.

 Load Case Report 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 3:10pm DEC 5,2016 
 Load Case Report $X 
 Inner and outer element temperatures are the same 
 throughout the model. No thermal ratcheting 
 calculations will be performed. 
 THE 6 LOAD CASES ANALYZED ARE: 
 1 WEIGHT ONLY (Wgt Only) 
 Weight ONLY case run to get the stress range 
 between the installed and the operating states. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 1 
 Loads due to Weight 
 2 SUSTAINED (Wgt+Pr) 
 Sustained case run to satisfy local primary 
 membrane and bending stress limits. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 2 
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 3 Thermal ONLY 
 Thermal ONLY case run in the event expansion 
 stresses exceed the secondary stress allowable. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 3 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Thermal Only) 
 4 OPERATING 
 Case run to compute the operating stresses used in 
 secondary, peak and range calculations as needed. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 4 

[page 539]
 Pressure Case 1 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Operating) 
 5 RANGE (Fatigue Calc Performed) 
 Case run to get the RANGE of stresses. 
 as described in NB-3222.2, 5.5.3.2, 5.5.5.2 or 5.5.6.1. 
 /-------- Combinations in Range Case 5 
 Plus Stress Results from CASE 4 
 Minus Stress Results from CASE 1 
 6 OCCASIONAL 
 Occasional load case established per the 
 requirements of the Code. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 6 
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 Loads from (Occasional)

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 3:11pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.7406250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 Thermal Stresses will be introduced into this analysis due to the 
 differences in temperature and/or thermal expansion coefficients. 
 The following temperatures have been specified for the analysis: 
 Nozzle Inside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 

[page 573]
 Nozzle Outside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Vessel Inside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Vessel Outside Temperature : 750.00 deg. 
 Nozzle Pressure : 320.0 psi 
 Vessel Pressure : 320.0 psi 
 Operating Pressure : 237.0 psi 
 The operating pressure is used for secondary and peak stress 
 cases. The design pressure is used for primary cases. The ratio 
 of the operating/design pressure = 0.741 
 No external forces or bending moments were included in this analysis. 
 Stresses ARE nodally AVERAGED. 
 The nozzle offset from the head centerline has been adjusted to 
 a value of 30.942 in. so that it will better fit on 
 the head. Please check the input carefully, and adjust the 
 design accordingly for this assumption. 
 Vessel Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Nozzle Centerline Vector: 1.000 0.000 0.000 
 Zero Degree Orientation Vector: 0.000 1.000 0.000 
 The location of the nozzle/plate on the head is being moved 
 slightly toward the middle of the head to allow for better 
 meshing near the outside radius of the head. The original 
 centerline location was 30.942 and the new 
 location is 29.705.

 Load Case Report $X 
 Inner and outer element temperatures are the same 
 throughout the model. No thermal ratcheting 
 calculations will be performed. 
 THE 6 LOAD CASES ANALYZED ARE: 
 1 WEIGHT ONLY (Wgt Only) 
 Weight ONLY case run to get the stress range 
 between the installed and the operating states. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 1 
 Loads due to Weight 
 2 SUSTAINED (Wgt+Pr) 
 Sustained case run to satisfy local primary 
 membrane and bending stress limits. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 2 
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 3 Thermal ONLY 
 Thermal ONLY case run in the event expansion 
 stresses exceed the secondary stress allowable. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 3 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Thermal Only) 
 4 OPERATING 
 Case run to compute the operating stresses used in 
 secondary, peak and range calculations as needed. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 4

 Temperature Case 1

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 3:43pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.7406250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 is 41.93 in.

 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 4:45pm DEC 5,2016 
 Load Case Report $X 
 Inner and outer element temperatures are the same 
 throughout the model. No thermal ratcheting 
 calculations will be performed. 
 THE 6 LOAD CASES ANALYZED ARE: 
 1 WEIGHT ONLY (Wgt Only) 
 Weight ONLY case run to get the stress range 
 between the installed and the operating states. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 1 
 Loads due to Weight 
 2 SUSTAINED (Wgt+Pr) 
 Sustained case run to satisfy local primary 
 membrane and bending stress limits. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 2 

[page 609]
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 3 Thermal ONLY 
 Thermal ONLY case run in the event expansion 
 stresses exceed the secondary stress allowable. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 3 
 Temperature Case 1 
 4 OPERATING 
 Case run to compute the operating stresses used in 
 secondary, peak and range calculations as needed. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 4 
 Pressure Case 1 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Operating) 
 5 RANGE (Fatigue Calc Performed) 
 Case run to get the RANGE of stresses. 
 as described in NB-3222.2, 5.5.3.2, 5.5.5.2 or 5.5.6.1. 
 /-------- Combinations in Range Case 5 
 Plus Stress Results from CASE 4 
 Minus Stress Results from CASE 1 
 6 OCCASIONAL 
 Occasional load case established per the 
 requirements of the Code. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 6 
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 Loads from (Occasional)

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 4:45pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.4306250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 3:51pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.4306250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 Load Case Report $X 
 Inner and outer element temperatures are the same 
 throughout the model. No thermal ratcheting 
 calculations will be performed. 
 THE 6 LOAD CASES ANALYZED ARE: 
 1 WEIGHT ONLY (Wgt Only) 
 Weight ONLY case run to get the stress range 
 between the installed and the operating states. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 1 
 Loads due to Weight 
 2 SUSTAINED (Wgt+Pr) 
 Sustained case run to satisfy local primary 
 membrane and bending stress limits. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 2 

[page 660]
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 3 Thermal ONLY 
 Thermal ONLY case run in the event expansion 
 stresses exceed the secondary stress allowable. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 3 
 Temperature Case 1 
 4 OPERATING 
 Case run to compute the operating stresses used in 
 secondary, peak and range calculations as needed. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 4 
 Pressure Case 1 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Operating) 
 5 RANGE (Fatigue Calc Performed) 
 Case run to get the RANGE of stresses. 
 as described in NB-3222.2, 5.5.3.2, 5.5.5.2 or 5.5.6.1. 
 /-------- Combinations in Range Case 5 
 Plus Stress Results from CASE 4 
 Minus Stress Results from CASE 1 
 6 OCCASIONAL 
 Occasional load case established per the 
 requirements of the Code.

 Loads due to Weight

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 4:00pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.4306250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.4306250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 Load Case Report 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 4:19pm DEC 5,2016 
 Load Case Report $X 
 Inner and outer element temperatures are the same 
 throughout the model. No thermal ratcheting 
 calculations will be performed. 
 THE 6 LOAD CASES ANALYZED ARE: 
 1 WEIGHT ONLY (Wgt Only) 
 Weight ONLY case run to get the stress range 
 between the installed and the operating states. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 1 
 Loads due to Weight 
 2 SUSTAINED (Wgt+Pr) 
 Sustained case run to satisfy local primary 
 membrane and bending stress limits. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 2 

[page 694]
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 3 Thermal ONLY 
 Thermal ONLY case run in the event expansion 
 stresses exceed the secondary stress allowable. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 3 
 Temperature Case 1 
 4 OPERATING 
 Case run to compute the operating stresses used in 
 secondary, peak and range calculations as needed. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 4 
 Pressure Case 1 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Operating) 
 5 RANGE (Fatigue Calc Performed) 
 Case run to get the RANGE of stresses. 
 as described in NB-3222.2, 5.5.3.2, 5.5.5.2 or 5.5.6.1. 
 /-------- Combinations in Range Case 5 
 Plus Stress Results from CASE 4 
 Minus Stress Results from CASE 1 
 6 OCCASIONAL 
 Occasional load case established per the 
 requirements of the Code. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 6 
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 Loads from (Occasional)

 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 4:19pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.4306250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 5:09pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.4306250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 Load Case Report $X 
 Inner and outer element temperatures are the same 
 throughout the model. No thermal ratcheting 
 calculations will be performed. 
 THE 6 LOAD CASES ANALYZED ARE: 
 1 WEIGHT ONLY (Wgt Only) 
 Weight ONLY case run to get the stress range 
 between the installed and the operating states. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 1 
 Loads due to Weight 
 2 SUSTAINED (Wgt+Pr) 
 Sustained case run to satisfy local primary 
 membrane and bending stress limits. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 2 

[page 745]
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 3 Thermal ONLY 
 Thermal ONLY case run in the event expansion 
 stresses exceed the secondary stress allowable. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 3 
 Temperature Case 1 
 4 OPERATING 
 Case run to compute the operating stresses used in 
 secondary, peak and range calculations as needed. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 4 
 Pressure Case 1 
 Temperature Case 1 
 Loads from (Operating) 
 5 RANGE (Fatigue Calc Performed) 
 Case run to get the RANGE of stresses. 
 as described in NB-3222.2, 5.5.3.2, 5.5.5.2 or 5.5.6.1. 
 /-------- Combinations in Range Case 5 
 Plus Stress Results from CASE 4 
 Minus Stress Results from CASE 1 
 6 OCCASIONAL 
 Occasional load case established per the 
 requirements of the Code. 
 /-------- Loads in Case 6 
 Loads due to Weight 
 Pressure Case 1 
 Loads from (Occasional)

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 5:15pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.4306250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.

 membrane and bending stress limits.

 Stress Results - Notes 
 FE/Pipe Version 7.0 Jobname: NOZZLE $P 
 Released Jul 2014 5:20pm DEC 5,2016 
 Stress Results - Notes 
 - Results in this analysis were generated using the finite 
 element solution method. 
 - Using 2013 ASME Section VIII Division 2 
 - Use Polished Bar fatigue curve. 
 - Ratio between Operating and Design Pressure = 0.4306250 
 Assume pressure increases all other stresses. 
 - Assume free end displacements of attached pipe are 
 secondary loads within limits of nozzle reinforcement. 
 - Use Equivalent Stress (Von Mises). 
 - Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation for operating stress. 
 Include S1+S2+S3 evaluation in primary case evaluation. 
 Assume bending stress not local primary for S1+S2+S3. 
 - Use local tensor values for averaged and not 
 averaged stresses.
